content_en:
The opinion which we mentioned is the best and easiest and it was delivered by “Umar bin Al-Khattab. The other opinion that a doubtful woman should stay until she reaches the age when she no longer menstruates is very difficult for people to adopt.

When a judge abrogates a woman’s marriage and her husband wants to return to her, is she entitled to a waiting period?

Q: A judge abrogates a woman’s marriage after giving birth to a baby because her husband no longer supports her financially for a long period of time. Three months later,

he wanted to return to her, is she entitled to a waiting period? It is well known that most women do not menstruate during the period of suckling the baby. Will she wait until the suckling period comes to an end and she menstruates once again?

A: All praise be to Allah. According to the unanimous agreement of the four Juristic Schools and others, she is entitled to a waiting period during which she must menstruate three times even if menstruation did not occur until the suckling period is over.

This is also the judgment of * Uthman bin ~ Affan and * Ali bin Abi Talib and none of the companions issues a different ruling. If the woman wants to give her baby to another woman to suckle him so that she may menstruate or even she has a medicine for that purpose, it is permissible to do so. Allah knows best.

A woman was divorced after giving birth to six

children. After divorce, she did not menstruate

for six months. Is it legal for her to marry

another man after this period?

Q: A woman used to menstruate when she was a virgin.

After her marriage, she gave birth to six children and

ceased to menstruate. She was divorced by her husband

while suckling her last baby. She stayed with her family

for six months and still she did not menstruate. Another

man proposed to her. They attended before a judge who 228

asked her about menstruation. She said that she had not menstruated years ago. The judge said: It is not legal for you to marry. Another judge made her marriage contract, but he did not ask her about menstruation. When a third judge was informed of the incident, he summoned the wife and the new husband, lashed him one hundred whips, told him that he had committed adultery and separated between them. The husband did not divorce. Is divorce effected?

A: All-praise be to Allah. If she no longer menstruates because of an illness or because of suckling a baby, she must wait until this reason is non-existent. If she does not know the reason for the cease of menstruation, she should wait for a year and then get married. This is the opinion of ~Umar bin Al-Khattab, Ahmed and Shafi*i. Thus her second marriage is invalid. The judge who separates between them is right. If we adopt the other opinion of Shafi and a judge made her marriage contract, no other judge has the right to separate between them. Besides, no

When a suckling woman has a medicine so that she may menstruate and she has already menstruated thrice, does her waiting period come to an end?

Q: When a divorced suckling woman felt that she did not menstruate a long time ago and she had a medicine so that

she may menstruate and she has already menstruated thrice, did her waiting period come to an end?

A: Yes, once she menstruated thrice, it came to an end. If she had a medicine so that she may menstruate and she has already menstruated, it is permissible. If she became hungry or tired and this led to menstruation, and she has already menstruated thrice, her waiting period came to end. Allah knows best.

A sick man divorces his wife but later denied that he had done so. Days later he died. Is the wife entitled to a death or divorce waiting period?

Q: A man got ill for a long time. He ordered his wife to get out of the house but she refused. Accordingly, he said: You are divorced. She got out and veiled herself. When he called her, she entered wearing her veil. When he asked her about the reason for such veil, she told him that he had divorced her. He denied and said that he had not divorced her. Days later, he died. Is she entitled to a death or divorce waiting period?

A: If he was fully aware during divorce, she is entitled to wait for both periods and she has the right to have her due share in his inherited properties. If he was absent-minded

and was not aware that he had divorced her, she is entitled _ to spend the death waiting period. Allah knows best.

Is a woman entitled to spend a death waiting period again if she did not spend it in her house?

Q: A woman used to get out of her house during the death waiting period out of necessity. Is she entitled to spend the waiting period again? Did she commit a sin?

A: Once four months and ten days passed after her husband’s death, the waiting period is over and she is not entitled to spend it again. If she was obliged to get out of her house during such period, she is not to be blamed for it. If she went out without any necessity, she should ask Allah for forgiveness and repent to Him, but she is not entitled to spend the waiting period again.

Is it permissible to engage a woman who spent just forty days out of the waiting period of her late husband?

Q: A man died and his wife spent forty days of his death waiting period. Then, she traveled to a faraway town. She

did not apply cosmetics or perfumes. Is it permissible to engage her?

A: All praise be to Allah. The death waiting period comes to an end after the lapse of four months and ten days. If there are still some days of this period, she should spend them in her house. She should not get out either at day or night except for a necessity. She should avoid applying cosmetics or perfumes either on her body or clothes. She is allowed to eat whatever food permissible. She can also meet any person permissible for her to meet. If aman proposed to her, she should not give him a clear-cut reply. Allah knows best.

If a wife intended to perform pilgrimage with her husband, but he died before travel, is it permissible for her to perform pilgrimage?

Q: If a wife intended to perform pilgrimage with her husband, but he died on Sha* ban, is it permissible for her to perform pilgrimage?

A: according to the unanimous agreement of the four Juristic Schools, it is not permissible for her to travel for pilgrimage during the death waiting period.

Suckling the baby When two sisters suckle the daughters of each other, is it prohibited for these daughters to marry their cousins?

Q: When two sisters suckle the daughters of each other, is it prohibited for these daughters to marry their cousins?

A: When a woman suckles another’s baby five times during the suckling period (the first two years), the (female) baby becomes her daughter. All the children of the suckling woman become brothers and sisters of this suckled baby whether they were born before or after suckling. None of the suckling woman’s sons should marry the girl suckled by his mother.

On the contrary, the suckled girl’s brothers can marry the daughters of the woman who suckled their sister, provided that such daughters were not suckled by their (the sons’) mother. Only the suckled girl is prohibited for the sons of the woman who suckled her.

Thus it is permissible for a man to marry the sister of his sister (by suckling) if he was not suckled by her mother and she was not suckled by his mother. As for the suckled girl, she should not marry any of the sons of the woman who suckled her according to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars.

The origin of this case is as follows:

The woman who - suckled this daughter becomes her mother. The mother’s sons are prohibited to marry this girl and they become her brothers. The woman’s brothers and sisters become her uncles and aunts. The woman’s husband becomes her father and his brothers and sisters become the girl’s uncles and aunts. The suckled girl becomes the daughter of the suckling woman who, in turn, becomes the grandmother of this girls’ children after her marriage. As for the suckled girl’s brothers, sisters, and parents, they are not affected by suckling according to the unanimous agreement of the four Juristic Schools.

Two men were suckled by the same woman, then one of them got married and he had a girl. Is it permissible for the other man to marry this girl?

Q: Two men were suckled by the same woman, then one of them got married and he had a girl. Is it permissible for the other man to marry this girl?

A: When a baby is suckled five times during the first two

years by a woman, she becomes his mother. All her

children, born before or after suckling him, are his

brothers. Suckling prohibits what birth prohibits as regards

marriage according to the Sunnah of the Prophet (peace Ix

upon him) and the unanimous agreement of Muslin: 234

scholars. Therefore, this man is not allowed to marry the other’s daughter just as he is not allowed to marry his brother’s daughter.

When a girl is suckled with her cousin, is it permissible for him to marry her sister?

Q: A man has two female cousins; one of them was suckled with him while the second was not. Is it permissible for him to marry the girl who was not suckled with him?

A: When a baby is suckled five times during the first two years by a woman, she is his mother and it is prohibited for him to marry any of her daughters born before or after he had been suckled, because they are his sister according to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars.

If a girl was suckled by a woman, she is not allowed to marry any of her sons. If the fiancé was not suckled by the mother of his fiancée neither was she suckled by his mother, it is permissible for them to marry each other according to the unanimous agreement of Muslim scholars. Allah knows best.

Is it permissible for the son of a suckling woman to marry the daughter of the girl who was suckled by his mother?
content_id:
Pendapat yang kami sebutkan adalah yang terbaik dan termudah, dan pendapat ini disampaikan oleh 'Umar bin Al-Khattab. Pendapat lain yang menyatakan bahwa seorang wanita yang meragukan haidnya harus menunggu hingga mencapai usia tidak lagi menstruasi sangat sulit untuk diterapkan.

Apabila seorang hakim membatalkan pernikahan seorang wanita dan suaminya ingin kembali kepadanya, apakah ia wajib menjalani masa iddah?

T: Seorang hakim membatalkan pernikahan seorang wanita setelah melahirkan bayi karena suaminya tidak lagi menafkahinya dalam jangka waktu yang lama. Tiga bulan kemudian,

suaminya ingin kembali kepadanya, apakah ia wajib menjalani masa iddah? Sudah diketahui bahwa kebanyakan wanita tidak mengalami haid selama masa menyusui bayi. Apakah ia harus menunggu hingga masa menyusui berakhir dan ia kembali mengalami haid?

J: Segala puji bagi Allah. Berdasarkan kesepakatan bulat keempat Mazhab Fikih dan lainnya, ia wajib menjalani masa iddah yang mana ia harus mengalami haid tiga kali meskipun haid tidak terjadi hingga masa menyusui berakhir.

Ini juga merupakan hukum yang ditetapkan oleh 'Utsman bin 'Affan dan 'Ali bin Abi Talib, dan tidak ada seorang pun dari kalangan sahabat yang mengeluarkan putusan berbeda. Jika wanita tersebut ingin menyerahkan bayinya kepada wanita lain untuk disusui agar ia dapat kembali haid, atau bahkan ia memiliki obat untuk tujuan tersebut, maka hal itu diperbolehkan. Allah lebih mengetahui.

Seorang wanita diceraikan setelah melahirkan enam

anak. Setelah perceraian, ia tidak mengalami haid

selama enam bulan. Apakah sah baginya untuk menikah

dengan pria lain setelah masa tersebut?

T: Seorang wanita biasa mengalami haid ketika ia masih gadis.

Setelah menikah, ia melahirkan enam anak dan

berhenti mengalami haid. Ia diceraikan oleh suaminya

sementara ia sedang menyusui bayinya yang terakhir. Ia tinggal bersama keluarganya

selama enam bulan dan masih tidak mengalami haid. Seorang

pria melamarnya. Mereka menghadap seorang hakim yang 228

menanyainya tentang haid. Ia menyatakan bahwa ia tidak mengalami haid bertahun-tahun yang lalu. Hakim berkata: Tidak sah bagimu untuk menikah. Hakim lain membuat akad nikahnya, tetapi ia tidak menanyainya tentang haid. Ketika hakim ketiga diberitahu tentang kejadian tersebut, ia memanggil istri dan suami baru itu, mencambuk suaminya seratus kali, memberitahunya bahwa ia telah berzina, dan memisahkan mereka. Suami tidak menceraikannya. Apakah perceraian itu terjadi?

J: Segala puji bagi Allah. Jika ia tidak lagi mengalami haid karena penyakit atau karena menyusui bayi, maka ia harus menunggu hingga alasan tersebut tidak ada lagi. Jika ia tidak mengetahui alasan berhentinya haid, ia harus menunggu selama satu tahun kemudian baru menikah. Ini adalah pendapat 'Umar bin Al-Khattab, Ahmad, dan Syafi'i. Dengan demikian pernikahan keduanya tidak sah. Hakim yang memisahkan mereka adalah benar. Jika kita mengambil pendapat Syafi'i yang lain dan seorang hakim telah membuat akad nikahnya, maka hakim lain tidak berhak memisahkan mereka. Selain itu, tidak ada

Apabila seorang wanita menyusui memiliki obat agar ia dapat mengalami haid dan ia telah haid tiga kali, apakah masa iddahnya telah berakhir?

T: Apabila seorang wanita yang dicerai dan sedang menyusui merasa bahwa ia tidak mengalami haid dalam waktu yang lama dan ia memiliki obat agar

ia dapat mengalami haid dan ia telah haid tiga kali, apakah masa iddahnya telah berakhir?

J: Ya, setelah ia haid tiga kali, masa iddahnya telah berakhir. Jika ia mengonsumsi obat agar dapat haid dan ia telah mengalami haid, hal itu diperbolehkan. Jika ia merasa lapar atau kelelahan dan hal ini menyebabkan haid, dan ia telah haid tiga kali, maka masa iddahnya telah berakhir. Allah lebih mengetahui.

Seorang pria sakit menceraikan istrinya tetapi kemudian menyangkal telah melakukannya. Beberapa hari kemudian ia meninggal. Apakah istri berhak atas masa iddah kematian atau masa iddah perceraian?

T: Seorang pria sakit dalam waktu yang lama. Ia memerintahkan istrinya untuk keluar dari rumah tetapi ia menolak. Oleh karena itu, ia berkata: Kamu dicerai. Ia pun keluar dan berhijab. Ketika suaminya memanggilnya, ia masuk dengan memakai hijab. Ketika suaminya menanyakan alasan hijab tersebut, ia memberitahunya bahwa ia telah mencerainya. Suaminya menyangkal dan berkata bahwa ia tidak pernah mencerainya. Beberapa hari kemudian, suaminya meninggal. Apakah ia berhak atas masa iddah kematian atau masa iddah perceraian?

J: Jika ia dalam keadaan sadar penuh saat menjatuhkan talak, maka ia berhak menjalani kedua masa iddah dan berhak mendapat bagiannya dari harta warisan. Jika ia dalam keadaan tidak sadar

dan tidak menyadari bahwa ia telah mencerainya, maka ia berhak menjalani masa iddah kematian. Allah lebih mengetahui.

Apakah seorang wanita wajib menjalani kembali masa iddah kematian jika ia tidak menjalaninya di rumahnya?

T: Seorang wanita biasa keluar dari rumahnya selama masa iddah kematian karena kebutuhan mendesak. Apakah ia wajib menjalani kembali masa iddahnya? Apakah ia berdosa?

J: Setelah empat bulan sepuluh hari berlalu sejak kematian suaminya, masa iddah berakhir dan ia tidak wajib menjalaninya kembali. Jika ia terpaksa keluar dari rumahnya selama masa tersebut, ia tidak dapat disalahkan atas hal itu. Jika ia keluar tanpa kebutuhan mendesak, ia harus memohon ampun kepada Allah dan bertaubat kepada-Nya, namun ia tidak wajib menjalani kembali masa iddah tersebut.

Apakah diperbolehkan meminang seorang wanita yang baru menjalani empat puluh hari dari masa iddah kematian suaminya?

T: Seorang pria meninggal dan istrinya telah menjalani empat puluh hari dari masa iddah kematiannya. Kemudian ia bepergian ke kota yang jauh. Ia

tidak menggunakan kosmetik atau parfum. Apakah diperbolehkan untuk meminangnya?

J: Segala puji bagi Allah. Masa iddah kematian berakhir setelah lewatnya empat bulan sepuluh hari. Jika masih tersisa beberapa hari dari masa ini, ia harus menjalaninya di rumahnya. Ia tidak boleh keluar baik siang maupun malam kecuali karena kebutuhan mendesak. Ia harus menghindari penggunaan kosmetik atau parfum baik pada tubuh maupun pakaiannya. Ia diperbolehkan memakan makanan apa pun yang halal. Ia juga dapat bertemu dengan siapa pun yang diperbolehkan untuk ditemuinya. Jika seorang pria melamarnya, ia tidak boleh memberikan jawaban yang tegas. Allah lebih mengetahui.

Jika seorang istri berniat melaksanakan haji bersama suaminya, tetapi suaminya meninggal sebelum keberangkatan, apakah diperbolehkan baginya untuk melaksanakan haji?

T: Jika seorang istri berniat melaksanakan haji bersama suaminya, tetapi suaminya meninggal pada bulan Sya'ban, apakah diperbolehkan baginya untuk melaksanakan haji?

J: Berdasarkan kesepakatan bulat keempat Mazhab Fikih, tidak diperbolehkan baginya untuk bepergian melaksanakan haji selama masa iddah kematian.

Menyusui bayi Apabila dua orang bersaudari menyusui putri-putri satu sama lain, apakah diharamkan bagi putri-putri tersebut untuk menikah dengan sepupu-sepupu mereka?

T: Apabila dua orang bersaudari menyusui putri-putri satu sama lain, apakah diharamkan bagi putri-putri tersebut untuk menikah dengan sepupu-sepupu mereka?

J: Apabila seorang wanita menyusui bayi orang lain sebanyak lima kali selama masa menyusui (dua tahun pertama), maka bayi (perempuan) tersebut menjadi putrinya. Semua anak dari wanita yang menyusui menjadi saudara dan saudari bagi bayi yang disusui tersebut, baik yang lahir sebelum maupun sesudah penyusuan. Tidak seorang pun dari putra wanita yang menyusui boleh menikahi anak perempuan yang disusui oleh ibunya.

Sebaliknya, saudara-saudara dari anak perempuan yang disusui dapat menikahi putri-putri wanita yang menyusui saudari mereka, dengan syarat putri-putri tersebut tidak disusui oleh ibu mereka (para putra). Hanya anak perempuan yang disusui itulah yang diharamkan bagi putra-putra wanita yang menyusuinya.

Dengan demikian diperbolehkan bagi seorang pria untuk menikahi saudari dari saudarinya (karena susuan) jika ia tidak disusui oleh ibunya dan ia tidak disusui oleh ibunya. Adapun anak perempuan yang disusui, ia tidak boleh menikahi seorang pun dari putra-putra wanita yang menyusuinya berdasarkan kesepakatan bulat para ulama Muslim.

Asal-usul kasus ini adalah sebagai berikut:

Wanita yang menyusui putri ini menjadi ibunya. Putra-putra ibu tersebut diharamkan menikahi anak perempuan ini dan mereka menjadi saudara-saudaranya. Saudara-saudara dan saudari-saudari wanita tersebut menjadi paman dan bibinya. Suami wanita tersebut menjadi ayahnya dan saudara-saudara serta saudari-saudarinya menjadi paman dan bibi anak perempuan tersebut. Anak perempuan yang disusui menjadi putri dari wanita yang menyusui, yang pada gilirannya menjadi nenek dari anak-anak perempuan ini setelah menikah. Adapun saudara-saudara, saudari-saudari, dan orang tua dari anak perempuan yang disusui, mereka tidak terpengaruh oleh hubungan susuan berdasarkan kesepakatan bulat keempat Mazhab Fikih.

Dua orang pria disusui oleh wanita yang sama, kemudian salah seorang dari mereka menikah dan memiliki seorang putri. Apakah diperbolehkan bagi pria yang lain untuk menikahi putri tersebut?

T: Dua orang laki-laki disusui oleh wanita yang sama, kemudian salah seorang dari mereka menikah dan memiliki seorang anak perempuan. Apakah boleh bagi laki-laki yang lain menikahi anak perempuan tersebut?

J: Apabila seorang bayi disusui lima kali selama dua tahun

pertama oleh seorang wanita, maka wanita itu menjadi ibunya. Semua

anak-anaknya, yang lahir sebelum atau sesudah menyusuinya, adalah

saudara-saudaranya. Persusuan mengharamkan apa yang diharamkan oleh

nasab dalam hal pernikahan berdasarkan Sunnah Nabi (semoga

keselamatan terlimpah kepadanya) dan kesepakatan bulat para ulama Muslim. Oleh karena itu, laki-laki ini tidak diperbolehkan menikahi putri saudara sesusuan tersebut, sebagaimana ia tidak diperbolehkan menikahi putri saudaranya sendiri.

Apabila seorang perempuan disusui bersama sepupunya, apakah boleh bagi sepupunya menikahi saudari perempuannya?

T: Seorang laki-laki memiliki dua saudari sepupu perempuan; salah seorang di antara keduanya pernah disusui bersamanya, sedangkan yang kedua tidak. Apakah boleh baginya menikahi perempuan yang tidak disusui bersamanya?

J: Apabila seorang bayi disusui lima kali selama dua tahun pertama oleh seorang wanita, maka wanita itu adalah ibunya dan diharamkan baginya menikahi anak perempuan wanita tersebut yang lahir sebelum atau sesudah ia disusui, karena mereka adalah saudarinya berdasarkan kesepakatan bulat para ulama Muslim.

Jika seorang perempuan disusui oleh seorang wanita, ia tidak diperbolehkan menikahi anak laki-laki manapun dari wanita tersebut. Jika calon suami tidak disusui oleh ibu calon istrinya dan calon istri pun tidak disusui oleh ibunya, maka keduanya boleh menikah satu sama lain berdasarkan kesepakatan bulat para ulama Muslim. Wallahu a'lam.

Apakah boleh bagi putra dari wanita yang menyusui menikahi putri dari perempuan yang disusui oleh ibunya?
content_ar: The opinion which we mentioned is the best and easiest and it was delivered by “عمر bin Al-Khattab. The other opinion that a doubtful woman should stay حتى she reaches the age عندما she no longer menstruates is very difficult for people to adopt. عندما a judge abrogates a woman’s marriage and her الزوج wants to return to her, is she entitled to a waiting period? Q: A judge abrogates a woman’s marriage بعد giving birth to a baby لأن her الزوج no longer supports her financially for a long period of time. Three months later, he wanted to return to her, is she entitled to a waiting period? It is well known that most women do not menstruate خلال the period of suckling the baby. Will she wait حتى the suckling period comes to an end and she menstruates حين again? A: All praise be to الله. وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of the four Juristic Schools and others, she is entitled to a waiting period خلال which she must menstruate three times even إذا menstruation did not occur حتى the suckling period is over. This is أيضاً the judgment of * عثمان bin ~ Affan and * علي bin Abi Talib and none of the الصحابة issues a different ruling. إذا the woman wants to give her baby to another woman to suckle him لذا that she may menstruate or even she has a medicine for that purpose, يجوز to do لذا. الله knows best. A woman was divorced بعد giving birth to six الأولاد. بعد divorce, she did not menstruate for six months. Is it legal for her to marry another man بعد this period? Q: A woman used to menstruate عندما she was a virgin. بعد her marriage, she gave birth to six الأولاد and ceased to menstruate. She was divorced by her الزوج بينما suckling her last baby. She stayed with her الأسرة for six months and still she did not menstruate. Another man proposed to her. They attended قبل a judge who 228 asked her about menstruation. Sقال that she had not menstruated years ago. The judge said: It is not legal for you to marry. Another judge made her marriage contract, but he did not ask her about menstruation. عندما a ثالثاً judge was informed of the incident, he summoned the الزوجة and the new الزوج, lashed him one hundred whips, told him that he had committed adultery and separated between them. The الزوج did not divorce. Is divorce effected? A: All-praise be to الله. إذا she no longer menstruates لأن of an المرض or لأن of suckling a baby, she must wait حتى this العقل is non-existent. إذا she does not know the العقل for the cease of menstruation, she should wait for a year and then get married. This is the opinion of ~عمر bin Al-Khattab, Ahmed and Shafi*i. وهكذا her ثانياً marriage is invalid. The judge who separates between them is الحق. إذا we adopt the other opinion of Shafi and a judge made her marriage contract, no other judge has the الحق to separate between them. بالإضافة إلى, no عندما a suckling woman has a medicine لذا that she may menstruate and she has already menstruated thrice, does her waiting period come to an end? Q: عندما a divorced suckling woman felt that she did not menstruate a long time ago and she had a medicine لذا that she may menstruate and she has already menstruated thrice, did her waiting period come to an end? A: Yes, حين she menstruated thrice, it came to an end. إذا she had a medicine لذا that she may menstruate and she has already menstruated, يجوز. إذا she became hungry or tired and this led to menstruation, and she has already menstruated thrice, her waiting period came to end. الله knows best. A sick man divorces his الزوجة but later denied that he had done لذا. Days later he died. Is the الزوجة entitled to a الموت or divorce waiting period? Q: A man got ill for a long time. He ordered his الزوجة to get out of the house but she refused. وبناءً على ذلك, قال: You are divorced. She got out and veiled herself. عندما he called her, she entered wearing her veil. عندما he asked her about the العقل for such veil, she told him that he had divorced her. He denied and said that he had not divorced her. Days later, he died. Is she entitled to a الموت or divorce waiting period? A: إذا he was fully aware خلال divorce, she is entitled to wait for both periods and she has the الحق to have her due share in his inherited properties. إذا he was absent-minded and was not aware that he had divorced her, she is entitled _ to spend the الموت waiting period. الله knows best. Is a woman entitled to spend a الموت waiting period again إذا she did not spend it in her house? Q: A woman used to get out of her house خلال the الموت waiting period out of necessity. Is she entitled to spend the waiting period again? Did she commit a الذنب? A: حين four months and ten days passed بعد her الزوج’s الموت, the waiting period is over and she is not entitled to spend it again. إذا she was obliged to get out of her house خلال such period, she is not to be blamed for it. إذا she went out without any necessity, she should ask الله for المغفرة and repent to Him, but she is not entitled to spend the waiting period again. Is it permissible to engage a woman who spent just forty days out of the waiting period of her late الزوج? Q: A man died and his الزوجة spent forty days of his الموت waiting period. Then, she traveled to a faraway town. She did not apply cosmetics or perfumes. Is it permissible to engage her? A: All praise be to الله. The الموت waiting period comes to an end بعد the lapse of four months and ten days. إذا there are still some days of this period, she should spend them in her house. She should not get out either at day or night except for a necessity. She should avoid applying cosmetics or perfumes either on her الجسد or clothes. She is allowed to eat whatever food permissible. She can أيضاً meet any person permissible for her to meet. إذا aman proposed to her, she should not give him a clear-cut reply. الله knows best. إذا a الزوجة intended to perform pilgrimage with her الزوج, but he died قبل travel, is it permissible for her to perform pilgrimage? Q: إذا a الزوجة intended to perform pilgrimage with her الزوج, but he died on Sha* ban, is it permissible for her to perform pilgrimage? A: وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of the four Juristic Schools, لا يجوز for her to travel for pilgrimage خلال the الموت waiting period. Suckling the baby عندما two sisters suckle the daughters of بعضهم بعضاً, is it prohibited for these daughters to marry their cousins? Q: عندما two sisters suckle the daughters of بعضهم بعضاً, is it prohibited for these daughters to marry their cousins? A: عندما a woman suckles another’s baby five times خلال the suckling period (the أولاً two years), the (female) baby becomes her daughter. All the الأولاد of the suckling woman become brothers and sisters of this suckled baby whether they were born قبل or بعد suckling. None of the suckling woman’s sons should marry the girl suckled by his الأم. On the contrary, the suckled girl’s brothers can marry the daughters of the woman who suckled their الأخت, بشرط أن such daughters were not suckled by their (the sons’) الأم. Only the suckled girl is prohibited for the sons of the woman who suckled her. وهكذا يجوز for a man to marry the الأخت of his الأخت (by suckling) إذا he was not suckled by her الأم and she was not suckled by his الأم. أما the suckled girl, she should not marry any of the sons of the woman who suckled her وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. The origin of this case is as follows: The woman who - suckled this daughter becomes her الأم. The الأم’s sons are prohibited to marry this girl and they become her brothers. The woman’s brothers and sisters become her uncles and aunts. The woman’s الزوج becomes her الأب and his brothers and sisters become the girl’s uncles and aunts. The suckled girl becomes the daughter of the suckling woman who, in turn, becomes the grandmother of this girls’ الأولاد بعد her marriage. أما the suckled girl’s brothers, sisters, and الوالدان, they are not affected by suckling وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of the four Juristic Schools. Two men were suckled by the same woman, then one of them got married and he had a girl. Is it permissible for the other man to marry this girl? Q: Two men were suckled by the same woman, then one of them got married and he had a girl. Is it permissible for the other man to marry this girl? A: عندما a baby is suckled five times خلال the أولاً two years by a woman, she becomes his الأم. All her الأولاد, born قبل or بعد suckling him, are his brothers. Suckling prohibits what birth prohibits as regards marriage وفقاً لـ the السنة of النبي ﷺ (peace Ix upon him) and the unanimous agreement of Muslin: 234 العلماء. لذلك, this man is not allowed to marry the other’s daughter just as he is not allowed to marry his الأخ’s daughter. عندما a girl is suckled with her cousin, is it permissible for him to marry her الأخت? Q: A man has two female cousins; one of them was suckled with him بينما the ثانياً was not. Is it permissible for him to marry the girl who was not suckled with him? A: عندما a baby is suckled five times خلال the أولاً two years by a woman, she is his الأم and يُحظر for him to marry any of her daughters born قبل or بعد he had been suckled, لأن they are his الأخت وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. إذا a girl was suckled by a woman, she is not allowed to marry any of her sons. إذا the fiancé was not suckled by the الأم of his fiancée neither was she suckled by his الأم, يجوز for them to marry بعضهم بعضاً وفقاً لـ the unanimous agreement of مسلم العلماء. الله knows best. Is it permissible for the son of a suckling woman to marry the daughter of the girl who was suckled by his الأم?
